claim: The framework test subset shall pass green under ANY active domain, or none, independent of which business domain is pinned.
why: Resolver doctrine (verdict #8, verbatim): 'Бизнес всегда должен думать, что фреймворк работает. Быть рабочим — ответственность фреймворка.' The framework must PROVE its health without depending on a particular business domain's content. This is the DIRECTIONAL guarantee the tiering (R-framework-suite-tiered) exists to serve. Not yet ported in the Go port: the Python-era mechanism partitioned the pytest suite into 'framework' and 'domain' tiers via a DOMAIN_COUPLED registry in spec/tests/conftest.py, making the framework tier selectable via `pytest -m framework` and runnable under a foreign active-domain pin (HOTAM_SPEC_ACTIVE_DOMAIN). The Go port uses `go test ./...` with no equivalent framework/domain tier partition -- there is no marker registry, no conftest collection-modification hook, and no mechanism to re-run the framework subset under a foreign domain pin. The proof-at-wave-boundary discipline is carried manually. PROSE (not ENFORCED): no enforcer exists; proving domain-independence requires a nested re-run under a foreign pin, not a single-run invariant.
